name: Enchiladas Verdes
procedure: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Sauté the chopped onion and garlic in a pan until softened.
Mix shredded chicken with 1 cup of enchilada sauce, onion, and garlic.
Warm the tortillas to make them pliable.
Fill each tortilla with the chicken mixture, roll them up, and place in a baking dish.
Pour the remaining enchilada sauce over the tortillas.
Top with sour cream and shredded cheeses.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Garnish with chopped cilantro before serving.

name: Chicken Parmesan
procedure: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Season the chicken breasts with salt and pepper, then dredge in flour.
Dip the chicken into beaten egg, then coat with a mixture of breadcrumbs and Parmesan cheese.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and brown the chicken on both sides.
Place the chicken in a baking dish, cover with marinara sauce, and top with mozzarella cheese.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the cheese is melted.
Garnish with fresh basil before serving.

name: Tacos al Pastor
procedure: Soak dried chiles in hot water until softened, then blend with vinegar, garlic, oregano, cumin, and cinnamon to make a marinade.
Marinate the pork slices in the chile mixture for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight.
Grill the marinated pork and pineapple slices until cooked and slightly charred.
Slice the pork and pineapple into small pieces.
Warm the corn tortillas and fill with the pork and pineapple mixture.
Top with chopped onion and cilantro, and serve with lime wedges.

name: Beef Stroganoff
procedure: Cook egg noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
In a large skillet, melt butter and sauté onions, garlic, and mushrooms until soft.
Add sliced beef to the skillet and cook until browned.
Sprinkle flour over the beef and stir well.
Gradually add beef broth while stirring, and bring to a simmer.
Stir in sour cream and Dijon mustard, and cook until the sauce thickens.
Serve the beef mixture over the cooked egg noodles, garnished with fresh parsley.

name: Vegetarian Lasagna
procedure: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Cook lasagna noodles according to package instructions.
Sauté garlic in olive oil until fragrant, then add zucchini and spinach. Cook until softened.
In a baking dish, spread a layer of marinara sauce, followed by a layer of lasagna noodles.
Add a layer of the ricotta cheese mixture, followed by the sautéed vegetables, and a sprinkle of mozzarella and Parmesan cheese.
Repeat layers, ending with a layer of noodles topped with sauce and remaining cheese.
Cover with foil and bake for 25 minutes, then remove the foil and bake for an additional 15 minutes until the cheese is golden and bubbly.
Garnish with fresh basil before serving.
